author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
nadīyāra ati, puṇyavatī pati⋅
vratāgaṇera ki manera gati
nija⋅putre mana, nāhi anukhana,
bhaṇe śacī⋅suta⋅carita⋅rīti

niśi śeṣa dekhi’, śayana upekhi’,
tila ādha nāhi dhairaya bā̐dhe
nānā dravye thāri, bhari sāri sāri,
laiyā cale dite nadīyā⋅cā̐de

śacīra gṛhete, praveśite cite,
uthalaye kata kautukasindhu
dekhaye sakale, jananīra kole,
khele vasi’ gorā gokula⋅indu

juḍāya nayana, nārīgaṇa⋅prāṇa,
pā’yā kole kari pāsare dehā
kahe narahari, āhā mari mari,
kebā sirajila ehena lehā
en_translation:
What is the state of mind of the very virtuous and devoted wives of Nadīyā? Their minds are not with their own sons for even a moment, as they praise the character and pastimes of the Son of Śacī.

Seeing the end of the night, forsaking their beds, they cannot hold patience for even half a moment. Filling plates with various items, row after row, they set off to offer them to the Moon of Nadīyā.

As they enter Śacī-mātā's house, a sea of joy overflows in their hearts. Everyone sees, in the lap of His mother, Gorā, the Moon of Gokula, playing.

Their eyes are soothed, and holding Him in their laps, the women forget their own bodies. Narahari says, "Oh, wonderful! Oh, wonderful! Who has created such a loving sentiment?"
